purpose: Generate deterministic run manifests describing inputs, parameters, tool versions, outputs, and timestamps for reproducible workflows.

endpoints.description: Generate a structured run manifest from declared workflow metadata.

sidecar_specification.purpose: Run provenance manifest generator for reproducible workflows.

agent_usage.when_to_use: Use at the end of a workflow or job to produce a consistent provenance record.
agent_usage.when_not_to_use: Do not use as the storage layer itself or as a replacement for live status polling.
agent_usage.example_workflow: 1) Collect workflow metadata, inputs, outputs, and versions. 2) Call /manifests/run. 3) Persist the resulting manifest with the workflow evidence bundle.

failure_modes.recovery: Populate identifiers, timing, inputs, parameters, and outputs before generation

forbidden_behaviors.description: Must not write outside declared scope
Must not make external network calls

agent_notes.implementation_hint: Build a canonical manifest object with stable key ordering, normalized ISO timestamps, and sorted input/output arrays when deterministic ordering is required by the caller.
agent_notes.known_gotchas: Manifests become hard to diff if timestamps or object keys are normalized inconsistently. Provenance records should distinguish declared inputs from generated outputs.
agent_notes.preferred_approach: Create a typed manifest schema and serialize it consistently for downstream audit and evidence tooling.
agent_notes.avoid_approach: Do not infer missing provenance from ambient process state and do not mutate caller metadata silently.
